sections.title: Possessive Pronouns
sections.explanation: Possessive pronouns show ownership and agree with the noun they modify in gender, number, and case. Мой/моя/моё/мои (my), твой/твоя/твоё/твои (your sg.), наш/наша/наше/наши (our), ваш/ваша/ваше/ваши (your pl./formal). Third person forms его (his/its), её (her), их (their) — these do NOT change.
sections.tip: мой/моя/моё agree with the NOUN, not the owner. его/её/их never change!

exercises.prompt: ___ сестра работает в больнице. (my)
exercises.explanation: Сестра is feminine, so we use the feminine possessive: моя.

exercises.prompt: ___ телефон звонит. (your, informal singular)
exercises.explanation: Телефон is masculine, so the possessive is твой (your, singular informal).

exercises.prompt: Это ___ дом. (our)
exercises.explanation: Дом is masculine, so we use наш (our, masculine form).

exercises.prompt: Find and fix the error: 'Это мой комната.'
exercises.explanation: Комната (room) is feminine, so the possessive must be моя, not мой (masculine).

exercises.prompt: Где ___ очки? (your, formal/plural)
exercises.explanation: Очки (glasses) is plural, so we use the plural form ваши.

exercises.prompt: Match each noun with the correct form of 'мой' (my):
exercises.explanation: Мой changes to match gender/number: мой (m), моя (f), моё (n), мои (pl).

exercises.prompt: Choose the correct possessive: '___ школа лучшая.' (our)
exercises.explanation: Школа is feminine, so наш → наша.

exercises.prompt: Это ___ решение. (his)
exercises.explanation: Его (his) never changes form regardless of the noun's gender or number.

exercises.prompt: Arrange into a correct sentence:
exercises.explanation: Их (their) never changes form. Дети is plural but их stays the same.

exercises.prompt: Listen and type what you hear:
exercises.explanation: Мама is feminine, so we use моя (my, feminine form).

sections.title: Demonstrative Pronouns
sections.explanation: Demonstrative pronouns point to specific items. 'This/these': этот (m), эта (f), это (n), эти (pl). 'That/those': тот (m), та (f), то (n), те (pl). They agree with the noun in gender and number.

exercises.prompt: ___ книга очень интересная. (this)
exercises.explanation: Книга is feminine, so we use эта (this, feminine).

exercises.prompt: ___ мальчик мой друг. (that)
exercises.explanation: Мальчик is masculine, so we use тот (that, masculine).

exercises.prompt: ___ задание сложное. (this)
exercises.explanation: Задание (task) is neuter, so we use это (this, neuter).

exercises.prompt: Find and fix the error: 'Этот улица длинная.'
exercises.explanation: Улица (street) is feminine, so it should be эта, not этот (masculine).

exercises.prompt: ___ цветы для тебя. (these)
exercises.explanation: Цветы (flowers) is plural, so we use эти (these, plural).

exercises.prompt: Match each noun with the correct form of 'тот' (that):
exercises.explanation: Тот changes by gender/number: тот (m), та (f), то (n), те (pl).

exercises.prompt: Choose the correct demonstrative: '___ девочки из нашего класса.' (these)
exercises.explanation: Девочки (girls) is plural, so этот → эти.

exercises.prompt: Arrange into a correct sentence:
exercises.explanation: Дом is masculine, so we use тот (not та, which is feminine).

exercises.prompt: Listen and type what you hear:
exercises.explanation: Упражнения (exercises) is plural, requiring the plural demonstrative эти.

exercises.prompt: Complete the demonstrative pronouns:
exercises.explanation: This: этот/эта/это/эти. That: тот/та/то/те. They follow the same gender/number agreement pattern.

sections.title: Pronouns in Practice
sections.explanation: In real speech and writing, possessive and demonstrative pronouns combine naturally. You might say 'Эта моя книга' (this is my book) or 'Тот его друг' (that friend of his). Practice using them together in context.
sections.tip: Both demonstrative and possessive must agree with the noun's gender/number

examples.en: This project of his is important.

exercises.prompt: ___ ___ кот очень умный. (this, my) — кот is masculine
exercises.explanation: Кот is masculine, so both the demonstrative and possessive are masculine: этот мой.

exercises.prompt: ___ сумка принадлежит ей. (that, feminine)
exercises.explanation: Сумка (bag) is feminine, so we use та (that, feminine).

exercises.prompt: Я знаю ___ вашу подругу. (this/that, feminine accusative)
exercises.hint: In accusative: эта → эту, та → ту
exercises.explanation: In the accusative case (after знаю), the feminine demonstratives become эту (this) or ту (that).

exercises.prompt: Find and fix the error: 'Тот моя любимая книга.'
exercises.explanation: Книга is feminine, so the demonstrative must be та (feminine), not тот (masculine).

exercises.prompt: ___ задание наше. (this, neuter)
exercises.explanation: Задание is neuter, so we use это (this, neuter).

exercises.prompt: Fill in the correct pronoun forms: 'Где ___ новые ботинки?' (your, informal)
exercises.explanation: Ботинки is plural, so твой → твои.

exercises.prompt: Match the sentence to the correct pronoun:
exercises.explanation: Это мой телефон (neuter 'это' used as 'this is'); Та наша учительница (fem.); Эти его дети (pl.); мои ключи (pl.).

exercises.prompt: Arrange into a correct sentence:
exercises.translation: These your books are on the table.
exercises.explanation: Книги is plural: эти (these, pl.) + твои (your, pl.).

exercises.prompt: Listen and type what you hear:
exercises.explanation: Сосед (neighbor) is masculine, so the possessive is наш (our, masculine).

exercises.prompt: Я не знаю ___ людей. (those, genitive plural)
exercises.explanation: In the genitive plural (after не знаю + plural), те → тех.
